steps.title: Spot what the sentence needs to complete
steps.content: The sentence starts with a noun phrase — “A theoretical relationship between tax rates and revenues” — followed by a comma and a blank. After the blank comes “in 1974”. The blank must introduce a clause that explains what happened to this theoretical relationship and when. From the passage context, we know Arthur Laffer introduced this concept by sketching the Laffer curve on a napkin in 1974. So the blank must describe that action.

steps.title: Choose the clause that maintains clear focus
steps.content:
C provides “the curve was famously sketched on a napkin by economist Arthur Laffer” — a clause where the subject is “the curve” (the visual representation of the relationship itself), which directly continues the thought about the theoretical relationship.

A and B shift focus to Arthur Laffer as the subject, creating awkward separation between the opening noun phrase and the new subject; D awkwardly focuses on the napkin (the tool) rather than the curve (the relationship itself).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
